column1: The British Broadcasting Company was formed in 1922. Its________________saw the sart of the first national radio broadcasting service in the UK. (INTRODUCE)
The company was______________owned at first, but in 1926 it changed into a public corporation. In other words, it became owned by the state. (PRIVATE)
John Reith was the Director General of the new corporation. He insisted that the BBC must be completely _____________rather than under the control of the government. (DEPENDENT)
Reith's aim was to broadcast radio, and later TV, programmes that were both informative and very__________ . (ENTERTAIN)
The BBC began regular TV broadcasts in 1934, and in 1964 opened a second TV channel, BBC2, renaming the__________channel as BBC 1. (ORIGIN)
Today, the BBC has millions of_______________and listeners all round the world. (VIEW)
As a young child, I used to help my grandmother in the kitchen. That's when I first became interested in________________ . (COOK)
I loved the____________of being part of a team working together in the kitchen, as well as handing, tasting and smelling the food. (FEEL)
Baking was always one of the most difficult things to get right. All the ingredients have to be weighed___________ . (ACCURATE)
There was nothing_____________or strange about the type of food I learned to cook with my gran. It was just old-fashioned, home-cooked food. (USUAL)
Nowadays, I cook food that is a bit more adventurous, but my gran taught me all the____________skills I need. (BASE)
Through her patience and her teaching____________ , she made sure that I learned to be a good all-round home cook. (ABLE)
